sanskrit:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते।
लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान् हि तान्॥
transliteration:
sa tayā śraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhate
labhate ca tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān
literal_meaning: He, endowed with that faith, seeks the worship of that (deity); from that he obtains desires, but they are indeed dispensed by Me alone.
contextual_meaning: People outsource their needs to external ‘quick-fix’ powers (money, status, substances, people) believing these idols grant results; unconsciously it is the Self that sanctions or withholds every outcome.  The verse exposes the projection: the ego attributes success to the proxy, hiding the inner authority.
psychological_state: Outsourced locus of control—anxious clinging to external validators while denying inner agency; sets up compulsive repetition of craving-gratification-disappointment cycle.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling through social media, seeing influencers promise ‘three easy steps to six-figure income’; you buy the course, get a dopamine spike, then blame the guru when results don’t last—never noticing it was your own discipline (or avoidance) that determined the outcome.
anchor_text: Every result you chase through idols is silently granted or denied by your own Self.
